kr.about.overview.title: Company Overview
kr.about.overview.p1: HicareNet is a digital healthcare company developing a SaaS platform for remote patient management under the US Medicare system.
kr.about.overview.p2: Built on over 20 years of remote patient monitoring technology accumulated by the healthcare division of its parent company, InSung Information, HicareNet was established as an independent entity through a spin-off in 2020. In 2021, the company set up a US subsidiary in California and launched Hicare Care Portal, a SaaS platform that supports RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) and CCM (Chronic Care Management) billing for US clinics.
kr.about.overview.p3: As of 2025, HicareNet has contracts with 40 US clinics, serves over 3,000 patients, and processes more than 300 insurance claims per month. With overseas revenue accounting for 96% of total revenue, the company operates a US-focused global business structure.
kr.about.mission.title: Mission
kr.about.mission.statement: In the US Medicare chronic care management market, we improve both clinic profitability and patient health outcomes through technology-driven billing automation. Our goal is to create an environment where clinicians can focus on patient care instead of administrative tasks.
kr.about.history.title: History
kr.about.history.2000s: InSung Information Healthcare Division begins R&D on remote patient monitoring technology
kr.about.history.va: Remote home care solution supplied for US veterans. Over 10,000 gateways (Hicare Hub) exported
kr.about.history.italy: Cardiac patient discharge management platform and 1,000 gateways exported to CEGLIE MESSAPICA Hospital
kr.about.history.france: 3,000 gateways exported to health service centers within a French pharmacy chain
kr.about.history.2020: Spin-off from InSung Information Healthcare Division; HicareNet, Inc. established
kr.about.history.2023: US RPM/CCM Care Portal service commercialized. Started with 15 clinics and 1,000 patients on the West Coast
kr.about.history.2025: 40 clinics, 3,000+ patients. H1 overseas revenue of $816,668. MoU signed with AMC Health

kr.business.title: Business
kr.business.care_portal.title: Hicare Care Portal
kr.business.care_portal.intro: A SaaS platform that automates RPM/CCM billing for US clinics.
kr.business.medicare.title: Medicare and Remote Patient Management
kr.business.medicare.p1: Medicare is a US public insurance program for individuals aged 65 and older and certain people with disabilities. Medicare reimburses clinics for remote health management of patients with chronic conditions.
kr.business.medicare.p2: RPM (Remote Patient Monitoring) is a service that remotely collects and analyzes patient vital data such as blood pressure and blood glucose. CCM (Chronic Care Management) is a non-face-to-face care management service for patients with chronic conditions. Clinics that provide these services can bill Medicare to generate revenue.
kr.business.medicare.p3: Care Portal is a SaaS platform that automates the entire billing process for these services.
kr.business.care_portal.problem.title: The Problem Clinics Face
kr.business.care_portal.problem.descript: To bill for RPM/CCM, clinics must document detailed deliverables including patient care records, monitoring data, consultation notes, and management time logs. This administrative burden is significant for clinicians, and incomplete documentation leads to missed claims.
kr.business.care_portal.solution.title: How Care Portal Solves It
kr.business.care_portal.problem1.title: Patient Care Workflow
kr.business.care_portal.problem1.desc: Patient enrollment, vital data monitoring, abnormal value alerts, consultation logging, and care plan management are all handled within a single platform.
kr.business.care_portal.problem2.title: Automated Documentation
kr.business.care_portal.problem2.desc: Automatically generates the deliverables required for RPM/CCM billing, including management time, consultation records, and monitoring logs. Reduces clinician administrative burden and prevents missed claims.
kr.business.care_portal.problem3.title: Billing Integration
kr.business.care_portal.problem3.desc: Automatically generates reports aligned with RPM CPT codes (99453, 99454, 99457, 99458) and CCM CPT codes (99490, 99487, 99489), linking them to Medicare insurance claims.
kr.business.care_portal.flow.description: HicareNet (provides Care Portal) -> Clinic (manages patients, performs monitoring) -> Patient (transmits vital data) -> CMS (reimburses insurance claims)
kr.business.care_portal.flow.cms_note: CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) is the US public insurance administration agency. The process is: clinics manage patients, document via Care Portal, then submit claims to CMS.
kr.business.care_portal.flow.device_note: Medical devices are FDA-approved devices owned by the clinics themselves. HicareNet does not supply or integrate devices.
kr.business.care_portal.feat1: Clinician Dashboard: Patient list, abnormal value alerts, priority-based sorting
kr.business.care_portal.feat2: AI-Powered Report Generation: Automatically generates documentation meeting CPT code-specific requirements
kr.business.care_portal.feat3: Time Tracking & Documentation Automation: Automatically logs call records, care plans, and management time
kr.business.care_portal.feat4: Multilingual UI: English, Spanish, and Korean
kr.business.care_portal.feat5: Patient App (Hicare App): Senior-friendly interface with voice-guided measurement prompts
kr.business.care_portal.revenue.title: Revenue Model
kr.business.care_portal.revenue.descript: A SaaS monthly subscription model. Clinics pay HicareNet a platform usage fee. Clinics use Care Portal to manage patients and bill Medicare for reimbursement. Revenue generation of approximately $50 per patient per month is possible (varies by condition and service type).
kr.business.care_portal.performance.clai: More than 300 insurance claims processed per month
kr.business.care_portal.performance.targ: 2025 target: East Coast expansion, 100 additional clinic contracts ($6,000,000 goal)
kr.business.expansion.title: Service Expansion Roadmap
kr.business.expansion.intro: Building on Care Portal as the foundation, we are expanding into the following areas.
kr.business.expansion.apcm.title: APCM (Advanced Primary Care Management)
kr.business.expansion.apcm.description: A new primary care-based chronic care management reimbursement program introduced by CMS in 2025. It extends the existing CCM model with CPT codes G0556, G0557, and G0558. Care Portal will support APCM workflows.
kr.business.expansion.awv.title: AWV (Annual Wellness Visit)
kr.business.expansion.awv.description: An annual health checkup program for Medicare beneficiaries. Requires completion of an HRA form and creation of a personalized prevention plan. Care Portal is preparing workflow automation for AWV.
kr.business.expansion.hra.title: HRA (Health Risk Assessment)
kr.business.expansion.hra.description: A required component of AWV for health risk evaluation. We plan to develop form automation and results analysis features.
kr.business.expansion.vbc.title: VBC (Value-Based Care)
kr.business.expansion.vbc.description: The shift from fee-for-service to outcomes-based reimbursement. After accumulating RPM/CCM/APCM data, we plan to evolve into a platform that supports contracts based on patient health outcomes.
kr.business.devices.title: Accumulated Medical Domain Expertise
kr.business.devices.intro: HicareNet has accumulated over 20 years of medical device integration technology since its parent company InSung Information era. This hardware and medical domain experience serves as the foundation for the current Care Portal software business.
kr.business.devices.hub.title: Hicare Hub (Gateway)
kr.business.devices.hub.description: A gateway product compatible with over 120 types of medical devices. It holds medical device certifications and has been commercialized for over 15 years, with exports to international markets.
kr.business.devices.export.va: US VA (Department of Veterans Affairs): Remote home care solution for veterans. Over 10,000 gateways exported. This US federal government project served as the foundation for US market entry.
kr.business.devices.export.italy: Italy: CEGLIE MESSAPICA Hospital. Cardiac patient discharge management platform and 1,000 gateways exported. Serving over 300 patients in the Puglia region.
kr.business.devices.export.france: France: Health service centers within a pharmacy chain. 3,000 gateways exported.
kr.business.devices.growth_story: Starting from hardware and device integration technology, the business has since transitioned to a software platform (Care Portal) focus.
kr.business.competition.title: Competitive Landscape
kr.business.competition.intro: The US RPM/CCM SaaS market includes players of various sizes.
kr.business.competition.100plus: 100Plus (under ConnectAmerica): Strengths in AI-based RPM/CCM and outsourced care management staffing
kr.business.competition.prevounce: Prevounce: Offers RPM, CCM, APCM, and AWV. Operates proprietary cellular devices + SaaS platform
kr.business.competition.vivify: Vivify Health (under Optum): Enterprise solution for large hospitals/health systems. FDA 510(k) cleared
kr.business.advantage.title: HicareNet Differentiators
kr.business.advantage1.title: Automated Billing Documentation
kr.business.advantage1.description: AI-powered report generation automates deliverables for each CPT code. Focused on reducing clinician administrative burden and preventing missed claims.
kr.business.advantage2.title: Multilingual, Multicultural Support
kr.business.advantage2.description: Supports English, Spanish, and Korean UI. Capable of serving multicultural patient communities in the US, including Korean and Hispanic populations.
kr.business.advantage3.title: Senior-Friendly Patient App
kr.business.advantage3.description: Designed with voice-guided measurement prompts and an intuitive interface so that elderly patients unfamiliar with digital devices can use it with ease.
kr.business.market.title: Market Size
kr.business.market.rpm_size: US RPM Market: $1.7B in 2023, projected $6.3B by 2030 (CAGR 18.4%, Grand View Research)
kr.business.market.ccm_size: US CCM Market: $2.2B in 2024 (CAGR 11.7%, Precedence Research)
kr.careers.title: Working at HicareNet
kr.careers.subtitle: We are a team building a SaaS platform that generates real revenue in the US Medicare market. It is rare to develop and operate a product targeting the US healthcare market from Korea.
kr.careers.why.title: Why HicareNet
kr.careers.why.1.title: US Healthcare SaaS Development Experience
kr.careers.why.1.description: You will directly build Care Portal, a patient management platform used by clinics under the US public insurance system, Medicare. The work involves domains rarely encountered in typical domestic projects: the CPT billing code system, HIPAA regulations, and multilingual senior-friendly UX.
kr.careers.why.2.title: 22-Person Team, High Individual Impact
kr.careers.why.2.description: A team of 10 R&D members builds and operates a platform used by 40 US clinics and over 3,000 patients. The organization is small, but the scope of real-world impact is broad.
kr.careers.why.3.title: Working to Global Standards
kr.careers.why.3.description: Collaborate with the US subsidiary and apply global security standards in practice, including HIPAA (US healthcare data protection), ISO 27001 (information security), and ISO 27701 (privacy management).
kr.careers.why.4.title: 96% Overseas Revenue
kr.careers.why.4.description: Revenue comes from international markets, not the domestic market. The products you build are directly validated in the global market.
kr.careers.team.title: Our Team
kr.careers.team.rnd.title: R&D Group (10 members)
kr.careers.team.rnd.description: The team that designs, develops, and operates Care Portal and the patient app (Hicare App). Covers the full software product lifecycle: backend, frontend, mobile, design, cloud infrastructure, and QC. R&D accounts for 45% of total staff.
kr.careers.team.bi.title: BI Group (7 members)
kr.careers.team.bi.description: Handles US market sales, business planning, government grant development, and clinical/product planning. Key responsibilities include expanding clinic contracts, planning new services (APCM, AWV, etc.), and identifying partnerships.
kr.careers.team.mgmt.title: Management Support (4 members)
kr.careers.team.mgmt.description: Responsible for HR, finance, accounting, and general affairs. Ensures stable operations for the 22-person organization.
kr.careers.team.overseas.title: Overseas Support Team
kr.careers.team.overseas.description: Supports operations of the US subsidiary (California), including management, HR, and accounting.
kr.careers.workstyle.title: How We Work

kr.careers.benefits.title: Benefits
kr.careers.positions.title: Open Positions
kr.careers.positions.always_open: Even if there are no current openings, we welcome applications from anyone interested in HicareNet. Please send your resume through the form below, and we will reach out first when a suitable position becomes available.
kr.careers.positions.no_openings: [PLACEHOLDER: To be updated after confirming current openings]
kr.careers.challenge.title: Problems We Are Solving
kr.careers.challenge.intro: HicareNet's R&D team uses technology to solve real problems in the US healthcare market. These are the challenges our team is currently focused on.
kr.careers.challenge.1.title: AI-Powered Report Generation
kr.careers.challenge.1.description: US Medicare RPM/CCM billing requires deliverables in specific formats for each CPT code. We are developing an AI pipeline that analyzes patient vital data and care records to automatically generate reports meeting each code's requirements. The goal is to reduce clinician administrative burden and prevent missed claims.
kr.careers.challenge.2.title: Multilingual Senior Usability Optimization
kr.careers.challenge.2.description: The primary users of the patient app (Hicare App) are patients aged 65 and older. The app supports English, Spanish, and Korean with voice-guided measurement prompts and an intuitive interface. The challenge is designing UX that accounts for varying digital literacy levels among elderly users from diverse cultural backgrounds.
kr.careers.challenge.3.title: Real-Time Vital Data Monitoring Infrastructure
kr.careers.challenge.3.description: We collect and analyze vital data, including blood pressure, blood glucose, and oxygen saturation, transmitted daily by over 3,000 patients in real time. We are working on improving the stability and scalability of the system that immediately alerts clinicians when abnormal values are detected.

kr.footer.disclaimer: HicareNet, Inc. is not a healthcare provider. HicareNet provides a software platform (Hicare Care Portal) that supports patient care workflows, documentation, and billing for clinics performing Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and Chronic Care Management (CCM) services under the US Medicare system.
